top_signals.title: U.S. Crude Inventories Fall 1.7 Million Barrels As Iran Tensions Rattle Markets
top_signals.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States saw a decrease of 1.7 million barrels during the week ending July 10, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The increase brings commercial stockpiles to 409.7 million barrels, according to government data, which are now 6% below the five-year average for this time of year. The EIA's data release follows API's figures that were released a day earlier, which reported that crude oil inventories saw a small draw of 564,000 barrels in the period.…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: European firms plan exo-atmospheric interceptor for 2027 test
top_signals.summary: Bliksem EXO is meant to defeat ballistic threats including Russia’s intermediate-range Oreshnik missile with separating and maneuvering re-entry vehicles.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: First the coast guard, then an ICBM: China tests new, long-term ways to hold off rivals in Asia
top_signals.summary: Experts say Beijing used both methods to try out new, attention-grabbing and long-term ways to deter others from challenging its territorial claims.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.summary: New report argues long-term reforms should focus on spending restraint, including end to pensions triple lock

top_signals.title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
top_signals.summary: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Ostium suffers $18 million exploit as oracle attack wave continues to hit DeFi
Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
top_signals.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.

alerts.title: Ostium suffers $18 million exploit as oracle attack wave continues to hit DeFi
Lessons Learned from CISA’s Recent GitHub Leak
alerts.summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued a postmortem on a data leak in which a contractor published dozens of internal CISA credentials -- including AWS Govcloud keys -- in a public GitHub repository for almost six months before being notified by KrebsOnSecurity. Experts say the gaps identified in the agency's initial response provide important lessons that all security teams should absorb.
